candidates.name: nasm_quote_filename
candidates.documentation: It is considered necessary if any one of these is true: 1. The filename contains control characters; 2. The filename starts or ends with a space or quote mark; 3. The filename contains more than one space in a row; 4. The filename is empty. The filename is returned in a newly allocated buffer.

candidates.name: free_mmacro
candidates.documentation: Free an MMacro

candidates.name: set_text_free
candidates.signature: static Token *set_text_free(struct Token *t, char *text, unsigned int len)
candidates.documentation: Set the text field to the existing pre-allocated string, either taking over or freeing the allocation in the process.

candidates.name: dup_text
candidates.documentation: Allocate a new buffer containing a copy of the text field of the token.

candidates.name: make_tok_num
candidates.documentation: Create a numeric token, with possible - token in front

candidates.name: make_tok_num_radix
candidates.signature: static Token * make_tok_num_radix(Token *next, int64_t val, char radix, bool uns)
candidates.documentation: Create a numeric token with specified radix and signedness; prefix the number with 0<radix> if a radix letter is specified, otherwise generate a decimal constant without prefix.

candidates.name: make_tok_qstr_len
candidates.signature: static Token *make_tok_qstr_len(Token *next, const char *str, size_t len)
candidates.documentation: Create a quoted string token

candidates.name: make_tok_char
candidates.documentation: Create a single-character operator token

candidates.name: new_Token
candidates.signature: static Token *new_Token(Token * next, enum token_type type, const char *text, size_t txtlen)
candidates.documentation: this function creates a new Token and passes a pointer to it back to the caller. It sets the type, text, and next pointer elements.

candidates.name: new_Token_free
candidates.signature: static Token *new_Token_free(Token * next, enum token_type type, char *text, size_t txtlen)
candidates.documentation: Same as new_Token(), but text belongs to the new token and is either taken over or freed. This function MUST be called with valid txt and txtlen, unlike new_Token().

candidates.name: steal_Token
candidates.documentation: This *transfers* the content from one token to another, leaving the next pointer of the latter intact. Unlike dup_Token(), the old token is destroyed, except for its next pointer, and the text pointer allocation, if any, is simply transferred.

candidates.name: zap_white
candidates.documentation: Delete any whitespace

candidates.name: free_tlist
candidates.documentation: Free a linked list of tokens.

candidates.name: free_line
candidates.documentation: Free a line

candidates.name: free_llist
candidates.documentation: Free a linked list of lines.

candidates.name: free_tlist_array
candidates.documentation: Free an array of linked lists of tokens

candidates.name: dup_tlist
candidates.documentation: Duplicate a linked list of tokens.

candidates.name: dup_tlistn
candidates.signature: static Token *dup_tlistn(const Token *list, size_t cnt, Token ***tailp)
candidates.documentation: Duplicate a linked list of tokens with a maximum count

candidates.name: dup_tlist_reverse
candidates.documentation: Duplicate a linked list of tokens in reverse order

candidates.name: new_mmacro
candidates.documentation: Allocate a new MMacro. This does not claim a refcount; the appropriate get_mmacro() calls need to be added.

candidates.name: free_mmacro_list
candidates.documentation: Unconditionally free a list of MMacros; used on final cleanup

candidates.name: free_smacro_members
candidates.documentation: Clear or free an SMacro

candidates.name: free_smacro
candidates.documentation: Free an SMacro

candidates.name: inc_fopen_search
candidates.signature: static FILE *inc_fopen_search(const char *file, char **slpath, enum incopen_mode *omp, enum file_flags fmode)
candidates.documentation: This is conducts a full pathname search

candidates.name: inc_fopen
candidates.signature: static FILE *inc_fopen(const char *file, struct strlist *dhead, const struct file_hash_entry **found_fhe, enum incopen_mode omode, enum file_flags fmode)

candidates.name: tlist_filename
candidates.documentation: Expand a token list that is expected to contain a filename string. Returns a new token containing a TOK_INTERNAL_STR with the given filename, or NULL on error. If the argument "*otp" is set, set that to point to the actual quoted string token.

candidates.name: pp_do_pathsearch
candidates.documentation: This implements the %pathsearch directive and %pathsearch() function. Returns a new token.

candidates.name: pp_do_depend
candidates.documentation: This implements the %depend directive and the %depend() function. It returns a stolen copy of the original string token after skipping leading spaces, or NULL on error. Returns a new token.

candidates.name: count_mmac_params
candidates.documentation: in a %macro definition line. Note that we need space in the params array for parameter 0 being a possible captured label as well as the final NULL. Returns a pointer to the pointer to a terminal comma if present; used to drop an empty terminal argument for legacy reasons.

candidates.name: smacro_expand_default
candidates.signature: static Token * smacro_expand_default(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: Default smacro expansion routine: just returns a copy of the expansion list.

candidates.name: expand_one_smacro
candidates.documentation: to point to the first token of the expansion, and *tpp updated to point to the next pointer of the last token of the expansion. If the expansion is empty, *tpp will be unchanged but **tpp will be advanced past the macro call. Return the macro expanded, or NULL if no expansion took place.

candidates.name: expand_smacro_with_params
candidates.signature: static Token * expand_smacro_with_params(SMacro *m, Token *mstart, Token **params, int nparam, Token ***epp)
candidates.documentation: Expand one single-line macro instance given a specific macro and a specific set of parameters. Returns a pointer to the expansion, and the pointer *epp pointing to the next pointer of the last token of the expansion; if the expansion is empty return NULL and *epp is unchanged. mstart is the token containing the token name *as invoked*.

candidates.name: use_mmacro
candidates.documentation: This is called from find_mmacro_in_list() after finding a suitable macro.

candidates.name: stdmac_ptr
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_ptr(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)

candidates.name: stdmac_default
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_default(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)

candidates.name: stdmac_strcat
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_strcat(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %strcat() function

candidates.name: stdmac_hs2b
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_hs2b(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %hs2b() function

candidates.name: stdmac_b2hs
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_b2hs(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %b2hs() function

candidates.name: stdmac_chr
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_chr(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %chr() function

candidates.name: stdmac_sel
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_sel(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %sel()

candidates.name: stdmac_cond
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_cond(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %cond()

candidates.name: stdmac_selbits
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_selbits(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparams)
candidates.documentation: %selbits()

candidates.name: stdmac_num
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_num(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)
candidates.documentation: %num() function

candidates.name: stdmac_abs
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_abs(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)
candidates.documentation: %abs() function

candidates.name: stdmac_map
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_map(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)
candidates.documentation: %map() function

candidates.name: stdmac_realpath
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_realpath(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)

candidates.name: stdmac_env
candidates.signature: static Token * stdmac_env(const SMacro *s, Token **params, int nparam)

candidates.name: nasm_quote
candidates.documentation: Create a NASM quoted string in newly allocated memory. Update the *lenp parameter with the output length (sans final NUL).

candidates.name: string_transform
candidates.signature: size_t string_transform(char *str, size_t len, char **out, enum strfunc func)
candidates.documentation: Apply a specific string transform and return it in a nasm_malloc'd buffer, returning the length. On error, returns (size_t)-1 and no buffer is allocated.

candidates.name: nasm_z_alloc
candidates.signature: static void *nasm_z_alloc(void *opaque, unsigned int items, unsigned int size)
candidates.documentation: read line from standard macros set, if there no more left -- return NULL

candidates.name: nasm_delete
candidates.documentation: This is broken on platforms where there are pointers which don't match void * in their internal layout. It unfortunately also loses any "const" part of the argument, although hopefully the compiler will warn in that case.
candidates.macro_value: do { \ void **_pp = (void **)&(p); \ nasm_assert_pointer(p); \ nasm_free(*_pp); \ *_pp = NULL; \ } while (0)

candidates.name: expand_buffer
candidates.signature: static void *expand_buffer(void *buf, size_t *bufsizep, size_t datasize, size_t elemsize)
candidates.documentation: Make sure there is enough space for at least one additional element

candidates.name: nasm_realloc
candidates.documentation: Unlike the system realloc, we do *not* allow size == 0 to be the equivalent to free(); we guarantee returning a non-NULL pointer. The check for calling malloc() is theoretically redundant, but be paranoid about the system library...

candidates.name: nasm_vaxprintf
candidates.signature: void *nasm_vaxprintf(size_t extra, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
candidates.documentation: The length of the string (with or without the final NUL) is available by calling nasm_last_string_{len,size}() afterwards. nasm_[v]axprintf() are similar, but allocates a user-defined amount of storage before the string, and returns a pointer to the allocated buffer. The size of that area is not included in the value returned by nasm_last_string_size().

candidates.name: hash_free
candidates.documentation: Free the hash itself. Doesn't free the data elements; use hash_iterate() to do that first, if needed. This function is normally used when the hash data entries are either freed separately, or compound objects which can't be freed in a single operation.

candidates.name: hash_free_all
candidates.documentation: Frees the hash *and* all data elements. This is applicable only in the case where the data element is a single allocation. If the second argument is false, the key string is part of the data allocation or belongs to an allocation which will be freed separately, if it is true the keys are also freed.

candidates.name: filename_set_extension
candidates.documentation: Add/modify a filename extension. Returns a newly allocated string buffer. If the extension is not an empty string, the first character is replaced by the appropriate extsep for the filesystem.

candidates.name: strlist_free
candidates.documentation: Free a string list. Sets the pointed to pointer to NULL.

candidates.name: elf_make_section
candidates.signature: static struct elf_section * elf_make_section(char *name, int type, int flags, uint64_t align)

candidates.name: ol_seg_alloc
candidates.documentation: Allocate a section index and add a section, subsection, or external symbol to the section-by-index table. If the index provided is zero, allocate a new index via seg_alloc().

candidates.name: _ol_get_sect
candidates.signature: struct ol_sect *_ol_get_sect(const char *name, size_t ssize, size_t rsize)
candidates.documentation: Find a section or create a new section structure if it does not exist and allocate it an index value via seg_alloc().

candidates.name: _ol_new_subsection
candidates.documentation: Start a new subsection for the given section. At the moment, once a subsection has been created, it is not possible to revert to an earlier subsection. ol_sect_by_index() will return the main section structure. Returns the new section index. This is used to prevent the front end from optimizing across subsection boundaries.

candidates.name: _ol_new_sym
candidates.signature: struct ol_sym *_ol_new_sym(const char *name, const struct ol_loc *v, uint32_t flags, size_t size)
candidates.documentation: If flags has OF_GLOBAL set, add it to the global symbol hash for the containing section if applicable. If flags has OF_IMPSEC set, allocate a segment index for it via seg_alloc() unless v->index is already set, and add it to the section by index list.

candidates.name: ol_new_sym
candidates.signature: static inline O_Symbol *ol_new_sym(const char *name, const struct ol_loc *v, uint32_t flags)

candidates.name: obj_clear
candidates.documentation: static bool obj_uppercase; /* Flag: all names in uppercase static bool obj_use32; /* Flag: at least one segment is 32-bit static bool obj_nodepend; /* Flag: don't emit file dependencies Clear an ObjRecord structure. (Never reallocates). To simplify reuse of ObjRecord's, .type, .ori and .parm are not cleared.

candidates.name: obj_emit
candidates.documentation: Emit an ObjRecord structure. (Never reallocates). The record is written out proceeded (recursively) by its previous part (if any) and followed (recursively) by its child (if any). The previous part and the child are freed. The main ObjRecord is cleared, not freed.

candidates.name: obj_emit2
candidates.documentation: Commit and Emit a record. (Never reallocates).

candidates.name: obj_new
candidates.documentation: Allocate and clear a new ObjRecord; Also sets .ori to ori_null

candidates.name: obj_bump
candidates.documentation: Advance to the next record because the existing one is full or its x_size is incompatible. Any uncommitted data is moved into the next record.
